title: landolphia × utilis
intro: Landolphia × utilis, also known as the 'West African yellow bark', is a hybrid plant species native to tropical Africa, resulting from the cross between Landolphia owariensis and Landolphia kirkii. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Landolphia × utilis include its potential to alleviate fever, reduce pain, and treat skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve using its bark in the form of infusions or decoctions, which can be taken internally or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
